# coreai-fabric

> The conversion fabric for Apple Core AI: recipes in, provenance-verified
> .aimodel out, indexed by coreai-catalog. Agent-first pipeline:
> new -> validate -> convert -> verify -> publish -> register. Fabric never
> hosts weights; artifacts go to each publisher's own Hugging Face namespace.

Key facts:
- Source of truth: recipes/*.yaml (one per artifact), schema/recipe.schema.json.
- Pipeline stages: draft, converted, verified, published, registered — advanced
  only by the corresponding CLI command.
- convert requires macOS + Apple's toolchain (apple/coreai-torch); CI never converts.
- Gate A = bundle structure checks (runs anywhere). Gate B = numeric parity vs
  upstream (cosine >= threshold from the recipe); reported not_run until a real
  runner measures it.
- register generates coreai-catalog entries: source_group fabric, huggingface-only
  artifact provenance with pinned revision + per-file sha256, provenance.converted_by.

## Docs

- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): the operating manual — full pipeline, exact commands, prerequisites, failure modes
- [README.md](README.md): positioning and ecosystem relationships (catalog / zoo / HF / Apple)
- [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md): contributions are recipes + parity reports; checkable rules
- [GOVERNANCE.md](GOVERNANCE.md): mergeable = validate green + license triage clean + upstream resolves + no digest conflicts
- [docs/parity-protocol.md](docs/parity-protocol.md): Gate B metric definitions and the runner contract

## Data

- [schema/recipe.schema.json](schema/recipe.schema.json): the strict recipe contract
- [recipes/](recipes/): recipe inventory (seed: qwen3-0.6b, whisper-large-v3-turbo, da3-small)

## Related

- [coreai-catalog](https://github.com/kevinqz/coreai-catalog): the registry that indexes fabric-published artifacts
